name: ZebPay Crypto Exchange API
what_it_does: Indian-origin cryptocurrency exchange with REST API — enabling traders and developers to access INR and USDT spot trading pairs, market data, order management, and account operations, with operations in India, Australia, and internationally, and one of the longest-running crypto exchanges in India (est. 2014).
use_cases: Accessing INR spot market data via ZebPay API for Indian cryptocurrency price tracking
Executing trades via ZebPay API for INR-denominated cryptocurrency algorithmic trading
Managing orders via ZebPay API for trading bot order lifecycle management
Fetching account balances via ZebPay API for Indian portfolio management applications
Integrating ZebPay market data via API for cryptocurrency analytics platforms
not_for: High-frequency trading with extensive altcoin selection (ZebPay has fewer pairs than CoinDCX)
Derivatives and futures trading (ZebPay is primarily spot)
Global exchange with full altcoin coverage (ZebPay focuses on major pairs + INR)
best_when: A developer or trader needs a long-established, regulated Indian exchange with INR trading and a track record of regulatory compliance across India and Australia.
avoid_when: You need extensive altcoin selection, futures/derivatives, or US market access.

auth.notes: API key and secret key. Signature: HMAC-SHA256 of request body using secret key. Headers: apikey (API key), signature (HMAC-SHA256). Timestamp parameter required. REST API at api.zebpay.com/. API documentation limited — PDF-based documentation. KYC required for all trading operations.

pricing.free_tier_limits: API access free; trading fees: 0.15% standard maker/taker
pricing.paid_tiers: Volume-based fee discounts for high-volume traders
pricing.notes: Founded 2014 in Ahmedabad, India — one of India's oldest crypto exchanges. Founders: Saurabh Agrawal, Sandeep Goenka, Mahin Gupta. Relaunch after India crypto uncertainty: ZebPay left India in 2018 when RBI imposed crypto ban, returned 2020 after Supreme Court ruling. Multi-jurisdiction: India, Australia, Singapore. Australian financial services license (AFSL). ~30+ trading pairs (smaller selection than CoinDCX). INR direct bank transfer. KYC: Aadhaar/PAN for Indian users. AUSTRAC registration for Australia. Regulation-first approach. PDF documentation (older format — less developer-friendly).

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: PDF documentation (not web-based). HMAC-SHA256 of request body. apikey + signature headers. Limited API coverage (~30 pairs). India-founded, returned 2020. Australian AFSL. KYC mandatory. Oldest Indian exchange.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: GET requests idempotent. Order placement: limited idempotency — check order status before retry. Market data reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: PDF documentation: primary API docs are in PDF format — less developer-friendly than web docs
Limited trading pairs (~30): ZebPay focuses on major pairs; less altcoin selection than CoinDCX
KYC mandatory: Aadhaar + PAN required for Indian users before any trading access
HMAC-SHA256 signs request body: ensure correct body serialization for signature calculation
Australia operations (AFSL): separate regulatory considerations for Australian users
API documentation may be outdated: ZebPay's API docs are less actively maintained than peers

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HMAC-SHA256 body signing. Mandatory KYC: strong identity verification. Indian FEMA/PMLA compliance. Australian AFSL: regulated financial services. One of India's oldest exchanges: proven security track record (no major hacks). Limited API surface: smaller attack surface. Secret key requires secure storage. PDF documentation: harder to verify security claims vs web documentation.
